query: someone just overdosed in front of me what do i do
reply:
**CALL 911 IMMEDIATELY** — this is a life-threatening emergency.

**While you wait for help:**
- Stay with the person.
- If you have Narcan (naloxone) available and know how to use it, administer it now.
- Place them on their side if possible (recovery position) to keep their airway clear.
- Do not leave them alone.

---

**After the emergency:**
If the person survives and wants support, **Coastal Horizons Quick Response Team (QRT)** helps overdose survivors and people at high risk:
- **Phone: 910-833-2052** (Wilmington area)

For immediate crisis support anytime, you can also:
- **Call or text 988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) — free, 24/7

**You did the right thing reaching out. Get emergency help now — call 911.**

Yes, Coastal Horizons offers therapy for anxiety and other mental health concerns. We provide:

- **Individual, group, and family counseling** for mental health disorders (including anxiety)

- **Services in Spanish** through Clinica Latina (910-769-1201)
- **Telehealth options** available

We serve **New Hanover, Brunswick, and Pender counties** with full mental health services, and we have programs in many other NC counties as well.

You can reach out through:
- **Online referral form**: https://coastalhorizons.org/services/substance-use-mental-health/new-patient-inquiries-and-referrals/
- **Call our main office**: 910-343-0145
- **Walk in** at our largest facility: 615 Shipyard Boulevard, Wilmington, NC 28412

No one is denied services due to inability to pay—we offer a sliding fee scale based on income and family size.
